title: cullmannia viperina
intro: Cullmannia viperina, also known as Viper's Bugloss, is a flowering plant in the borage family, native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating wounds and infections. The health benefits of Cullmannia viperina include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and act as an expectorant, helping to relieve respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or ointment, and is often used in herbal remedies to support the immune system and promote overall well-being.
